title: SEDE: Structural Entropy Dark Energy — reproduction code and pipeline
description: Code, data vectors, and the analysis pipeline that reproduce the results and figures of both SEDE papers: Paper I, "Structural Entropy Dark Energy: a fixed-parameter, growth-gated holographic dark-energy model without a cosmological constant" (cosmology), and Paper II, "Is the volume-law horizon a postulate or a consequence? A driven non-equilibrium reduction of Structural Entropy Dark Energy" (foundations companion). A single entry point (reproduce_all.py) regenerates every number and figure; the model and likelihood live in the sede/ package, the analysis scripts in experiments/, and a verification suite checks the quoted results numerically. SEDE is a cosmological-constant-free dark-energy model in which dark energy is the thermodynamic conjugate of the cosmic apparent-horizon entropy, gated by a structure-growth factor; Paper II reduces its volume-law horizon postulate toward first principles via horizon thermodynamics, holography, and non-equilibrium statistical mechanics.
